mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a type of operating system structure?
mcqs.explanation: Monolithic, Microkernel, and Layered are recognized OS structure types. Distributed refers to systems spread across networks, not a kernel architecture type.

mcqs.question: In which OS structure are device drivers typically part of the kernel?
mcqs.explanation: In monolithic kernel structure, device drivers run in kernel space as part of the single large kernel image.

mcqs.question: What is the primary advantage of microkernel over monolithic kernel?
mcqs.explanation: Microkernel provides better fault isolation since services run in separate user-space processes, so one crashing service doesn't crash the entire system.

mcqs.question: Which system call is used to create a new process in UNIX?
mcqs.explanation: The fork() system call creates a new process by duplicating the calling process.

mcqs.question: The layered approach in OS design follows which principle?
mcqs.explanation: Layered design uses abstraction, encapsulation, and information hiding to create clean interfaces between layers.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a Linux kernel characteristic?
mcqs.explanation: Linux uses a monolithic kernel with loadable modules (LKM) that can be dynamically loaded/unloaded.

mcqs.question: What is the main function of the system call interface?

options.text: To provide controlled access to kernel services

mcqs.explanation: System call interface acts as a boundary between user programs and kernel, providing controlled access to privileged operations.

mcqs.question: Which Windows NT kernel architecture is an example of?
mcqs.explanation: Windows NT uses a hybrid kernel design combining aspects of both monolithic and microkernel architectures.

mcqs.question: What happens when a system call is invoked?

options.text: The user program directly accesses hardware
A software interrupt (trap) is generated to switch to kernel mode
A new process is created automatically

mcqs.explanation: System calls use software interrupts/traps to transfer control from user mode to kernel mode safely.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a component of the OS kernel?
mcqs.explanation: User applications run in user mode and are not part of the kernel, which handles core system functions.

mcqs.question: What is the main drawback of monolithic kernel structure?

options.text: Poor performance due to excessive context switching
Single point of failure - entire OS crashes if kernel crashes

mcqs.explanation: Monolithic kernels run all services in single address space, so a bug can crash the entire system.

mcqs.question: The MINIX 3 operating system is an example of which architecture?
mcqs.explanation: MINIX 3 uses a microkernel design with most services running as separate user-level server processes.

mcqs.question: Which of these is an example of a command-line shell?
mcqs.explanation: Bash (Bourne Again Shell) is a command-line interpreter/shell used in Unix-like systems.

mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of the bootloader?

options.text: To load the OS kernel into memory and initialize hardware

mcqs.explanation: Bootloader initializes basic hardware and loads the OS kernel from storage into RAM.

mcqs.question: In a client-server OS model, which component handles requests?
mcqs.explanation: Server processes handle service requests from client processes in client-server architecture.

mcqs.question: What is the main advantage of loadable kernel modules (LKM)?

options.text: They allow adding/removing functionality without rebooting
They eliminate the need for drivers
They run in user mode always

mcqs.explanation: LKMs allow dynamic loading/unloading of drivers and functionality at runtime without system restart.

mcqs.question: Which mode has unrestricted access to hardware in traditional OS design?
mcqs.explanation: Kernel mode or privileged mode allows execution of privileged instructions and direct hardware access.

mcqs.question: What type of OS structure does Android use?

options.text: Linux monolithic kernel with proprietary user space
No kernel - runs directly on hardware

mcqs.explanation: Android uses Linux kernel (monolithic with modules) with a custom Java-based user space stack.

mcqs.question: Which OS component manages inter-process communication (IPC)?
mcqs.explanation: The kernel provides IPC mechanisms like pipes, message queues, shared memory for process communication.

mcqs.question: The concept of virtual machines allows:

options.text: Running only one OS at a time
Multiple isolated OS instances on single hardware
Eliminating the need for any OS

mcqs.explanation: Virtual machine architecture enables multiple virtual machines, each running its own OS, on a single physical machine.
